# Enterprise SaaS Agreement

**Effective Date:** 15 March 2026  
**Provider:** Nimbus Analytics Pte. Ltd.  
**Customer:** Harbor Retail Group Ltd.

This Enterprise SaaS Master Subscription Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into by and between Provider and Customer as of the Effective Date.

## 1. Commercial Terms

### 1.1 Access Scope
Subject to this Agreement and each applicable Order Form, Provider grants Customer a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable right during the Subscription Term to access and use the Subscription Services in production and staging environments for Customer's internal business operations.

#### 1.1.1 User Controls
Customer shall ensure each Authorized User has unique credentials, enable multi-factor authentication where available, and apply role-based access controls consistent with least-privilege principles.

### 1.2 Order Priority
Order Forms define subscription quantities, usage metrics, commercial terms, and service components. Any inconsistent Customer procurement or purchase-order terms are rejected unless expressly accepted in writing by Provider. If an Order Form and this Agreement conflict, the Order Form prevails only for commercial variables expressly identified in that Order Form.

#### 1.2.1 Change Controls
Any expansion of scope, additional modules, seat increases, or materially new integrations must be documented in a signed change order or replacement Order Form, and related fees are billed pro rata unless otherwise stated.

### 1.3 Invoicing Terms
Provider may invoice according to the billing cadence in the applicable Order Form. Invoices are due within thirty (30) days of invoice date. Fees are exclusive of taxes unless explicitly stated otherwise.

Provider shall include line-item invoice summaries covering recurring subscription fees, variable usage charges, and one-off professional services charges.

#### 1.3.1 Fee Disputes
Customer shall raise good-faith invoice disputes within ten (10) business days and pay all undisputed amounts when due. The parties shall escalate unresolved disputes to commercial governance contacts.

## 2. Service Operations

### 2.1 SLA Measurement
Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain 99.9% monthly uptime, excluding scheduled maintenance windows, emergency maintenance, force majeure, and third-party network failures outside Provider's reasonable control.

Provider shall provide monthly SLA performance reports and identify excluded downtime categories used in uptime calculations.

#### 2.1.1 Credit Remedies
If uptime falls below the stated target in a calendar month, Customer may request service credits under the SLA schedule. Except where explicitly stated otherwise, such credits are Customer's sole monetary remedy for that SLA shortfall.

Repeated SLA failures across three (3) consecutive months constitute material breach for purposes of Section 4.4.

### 2.2 Incident Support
Provider shall maintain support channels and response targets by incident severity, including continuous support coverage for Severity 1 production incidents.

#### 2.2.1 Incident Governance
For major incidents, each party shall appoint an incident manager, maintain a shared event timeline, and conduct a post-incident review with corrective and preventive actions.

For Severity 1 incidents, Provider shall issue status updates no less frequently than every sixty (60) minutes until service restoration.

### 2.3 Suspension Controls
Provider may suspend affected access where necessary to address verified security abuse, legal non-compliance, or sustained non-payment after written notice where practicable.

Provider shall maintain an internal suspension log containing timestamp, trigger, scope, and restoration actions, and provide a summary to Customer upon reasonable request.

#### 2.3.1 Limited Suspension
Provider shall use commercially reasonable efforts to limit suspension to affected accounts, credentials, or tenants and preserve safe read-only data export paths where possible.

## 3. Data Security

### 3.1 Data Processing
Provider shall process Customer Data solely for service delivery, maintenance, support, and lawful operational purposes as documented in this Agreement and the Data Processing Addendum.

#### 3.1.1 Cross-border Transfers
Where Customer Data is transferred across borders, the parties shall implement legally valid transfer mechanisms, including standard contractual clauses where required by applicable law.

Where transfer impact assessments are required by applicable law, Provider shall provide commercially reasonable assistance and relevant transfer documentation.

### 3.2 Security Audits
Provider shall maintain a risk-based information security program including access control, logging, vulnerability management, encryption in transit and at rest, and independent periodic testing.

#### 3.2.1 Audit Rights
No more than once per contract year, Customer may request a reasonable set of security attestations and summary audit artifacts, subject to confidentiality and third-party contractual limitations.

### 3.3 Incident Response
Provider shall notify Customer without undue delay and within seventy-two (72) hours after confirming a Security Incident impacting Customer Data, including known scope, preliminary impact, and immediate containment actions.

#### 3.3.1 Remediation Duties
Provider shall provide ongoing incident status updates, root cause findings, and preventive action plans until closure, and reasonably cooperate with Customer's lawful regulatory support requests.

Unless otherwise agreed for unusually complex incidents, Provider shall provide a written root-cause analysis within ten (10) business days after containment.

## 4. IP Liability

### 4.1 Output Ownership
Provider retains ownership of platform software, models, documentation, and related intellectual property. Customer retains ownership of Customer Data and customer-specific outputs generated from Customer workflows.

#### 4.1.1 Output Allocation
As between the parties, Customer owns outputs generated from Customer prompts and data, while Provider retains rights in underlying models, orchestration methods, and non-customer-specific improvements.

### 4.2 Warranties Indemnity
Each party warrants authority to enter this Agreement. Provider warrants material conformity to documentation. Except for express warranties, services are provided on an "as is" basis to the maximum extent permitted by law.

#### 4.2.1 IP Remedies
For covered third-party IP claims, Provider may (a) modify the services to be non-infringing, (b) procure rights for continued use, or (c) terminate affected services and refund prepaid unused fees.

### 4.3 Liability Caps
Except for excluded liabilities, each party's aggregate liability shall not exceed one hundred and twenty percent (120%) of fees paid by Customer under this Agreement in the twelve (12) months preceding the triggering event.

For Security Incident claims involving unauthorized disclosure of Customer Data, Provider's aggregate liability cap is increased to one hundred and fifty percent (150%) of fees paid by Customer under this Agreement in the twelve (12) months preceding the triggering event.

#### 4.3.1 Exclusions
The liability cap does not limit obligations arising from fraud, willful misconduct, confidentiality breach, data protection obligations, or Customer's unpaid fees.

### 4.4 Data Exit
This Agreement remains in effect while any Order Form is active. Either party may terminate for material breach not cured within thirty (30) days after written notice.

#### 4.4.1 Deletion Timeline
For sixty (60) days after termination, Customer may export Customer Data in a commonly used machine-readable format. Provider shall delete Customer Data from active systems within ninety (90) days, unless legal retention obligations apply.

Upon Customer request, Provider shall issue written deletion certification identifying retained datasets (if any) and the legal basis for retention.

## 5. General Provisions

### 5.1 Governing Law
This Agreement is governed by the laws of Singapore, without regard to conflict-of-law principles.

### 5.2 Dispute Arbitration
Disputes not resolved through executive escalation shall be resolved by arbitration in Singapore under SIAC Rules, in English, by one arbitrator.

### 5.3 Agreement Amendments
This Agreement and executed Order Forms constitute the complete agreement regarding the subject matter. Amendments require written signature by authorized representatives of both parties.

### 5.4 Assignment Limits
Neither party may assign this Agreement without prior written consent, except to an affiliate or as part of merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all assets, provided obligations are assumed.
